findings.title: ModelRouter exported but has zero consumers outside tests
findings.description: ModelRouter, classifyTask, and all related types are exported from the package barrel (index.ts) but are not imported or used by any consumer in apps/ or other packages/. The only references are within the context-engine package itself (definition, tests, re-exports). This is orphan code -- it cannot be exercised from any live entry point (HTTP handler, Electron IPC, event listener, cron job). Deploying this change alone does nothing to the running application.
findings.suggestion: Wire ModelRouter into the orchestrator at apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/main/services/anima/orchestrator.ts as a drop-in LlmProvider replacement. Until a consumer exists, this is dead code. Even a minimal integration (constructor wiring + one call path exercised in an integration test) would resolve this.

findings.title: DeduplicationTracker exported but has zero consumers outside tests
findings.description: DeduplicationTracker is exported from the package barrel but not imported by any code outside the context-engine test files. ScreenshotPipeline (the intended consumer per the JSDoc) does not use it. No app-level code references it. This is orphan code -- the deduplication statistics this class produces cannot be observed at runtime.
findings.suggestion: Integrate DeduplicationTracker into ScreenshotPipeline so that every pHash comparison is tracked. Expose stats via a getter so the orchestrator or a settings panel can display dedup effectiveness. Without a live consumer, this class provides no value.

findings.title: ModelRouter routing stats stored only in memory, lost on restart
findings.code_snippet:
private tierCalls: Record<ModelTier, number> = { lightweight: 0, standard: 0, local: 0 }
private taskCalls: Record<TaskType, number> = { classification: 0, extraction: 0, generation: 0, summarization: 0 }
findings.description: The routing statistics (callsByTier, callsByTaskType, estimatedSavingsRatio) exist only as in-memory counters on the ModelRouter instance. If the Electron app restarts, all cost-tracking data is lost. For a feature whose purpose is cost control and observability, non-persistent counters defeat the goal. The user cannot view historical cost trends or verify savings over time.
findings.suggestion: Persist routing stats to the existing SQLite store at meaningful intervals (e.g., on getStats() or periodically). At minimum, emit the stats to a structured log sink so they survive restarts. The persistence layer already exists in the context-engine (DocumentStore/VectorStore use SQLite).

findings.title: ModelRouter.generateText/generateStructured lack try/catch on LLM I/O
findings.description: Both generateText() and generateStructured() call external LLM providers (network I/O) without any try/catch. Raw provider errors propagate without routing context -- a caller seeing 'Model overloaded' has no idea which tier, task type, or provider failed. This is the same recurring pattern as AiOrchestrator, capture modules, and channel modules. Additionally, the test titled 'propagates provider errors with routing context' is misleading: it only asserts the raw error message, confirming no context is actually added.

findings.title: Routing stats incremented before I/O -- corrupted on provider failure
findings.description: In both generateText() (lines 140-141) and generateStructured() (lines 155-156), tierCalls and taskCalls counters are incremented BEFORE the async provider call. If the provider throws, the stats record a call that never completed. This corrupts the estimatedSavingsRatio and callsByTier/callsByTaskType metrics, which are the core deliverable of this performance/cost-control task. The cost estimation tests all use non-failing providers so this bug is not caught.
findings.suggestion:
Move stat increments after the await, or use try/finally to only count on success:

const result = await provider.generateText(options)
this.tierCalls[tier]++
this.taskCalls[taskType]++
return result

Alternatively, track both attempted and succeeded counts for observability.

findings.title: Comment claims extraction routes to lightweight, but it routes to local
findings.description: The comment on line 147 states that entity extraction routes to the 'lightweight' tier. However, the ModelRouter's resolveTier function routes extraction tasks to the 'local' tier when a local provider is present (extraction is in LOCAL_ELIGIBLE_TASKS). The test assertions on lines 175-176 correctly show callsByTier.local === 120 (both classification AND extraction) and callsByTier.lightweight === 0, contradicting the comment. The cost breakdown on lines 179-180 is also correct ('Local: 120 calls'). Only this one line misattributes the extraction routing destination.
findings.suggestion: Change line 147 to: '// - Entity extraction (60 calls) -> local: "Extract entities..."' to match the actual routing behavior and be consistent with the assertions and cost breakdown below it.

findings.title: Routing override to 'local' silently falls back to lightweight
findings.description: If a user configures routingOverrides: { summarization: 'local' } but does not provide a local provider, resolveTier returns 'local' and getProvider silently falls back to the lightweight provider. The caller has no way to know their explicit override was not honored. This violates fail-fast principles -- misconfiguration should be surfaced, not silently degraded.
findings.suggestion: Validate routingOverrides in the constructor: if any override targets 'local' but options.providers.local is undefined, throw an Error with a clear message. Alternatively, log a warning when getProvider falls back from local to lightweight.

findings.title: ModelRouter has no structured logging for routing decisions
findings.description: ModelRouter makes routing decisions (task classification, tier selection) without any structured logging. For a cost-control component, observability of routing decisions is critical for debugging misclassifications and verifying cost savings. The existing pattern in the codebase uses @guiiai/logg (useLogg). Other context-engine classes lack logging too (context-merger has a TODO-like comment about it), but for a cost-control feature, this gap is more impactful.
findings.suggestion: Add structured logging at DEBUG level for each routing decision: { taskType, tier, system: options.system.slice(0, 80) }. Use @guiiai/logg useLogg pattern consistent with other packages in the monorepo.

findings.title: Change is horizontal-only: no end-to-end path exercised
findings.code_snippet:
export { classifyTask, ModelRouter } from './processing/model-router'
export type { ModelRouterOptions, ModelTier, RoutingStats, TaskType } from './processing/model-router'
findings.description: This task adds two new modules (ModelRouter, DeduplicationTracker) with unit tests and benchmark tests, but no vertical slice connects them to the running application. The orchestrator in apps/stage-tamagotchi still creates LlmProvider instances directly without ModelRouter. The ScreenshotPipeline still does not use DeduplicationTracker. No integration test proves the router works within the actual context merge pipeline. The change is entirely horizontal: new library code with no wiring to any entry point.
findings.suggestion: Deliver a minimal vertical slice: (1) Wire ModelRouter into the orchestrator as the LlmProvider passed to ContextMerger/EntityExtractor. (2) Wire DeduplicationTracker into ScreenshotPipeline. (3) Add one integration test proving a context merge flows through ModelRouter end-to-end.

findings.title: ModelRouter does not add routing context to provider errors
findings.description: When a downstream provider throws (e.g., 'Model overloaded'), the error propagates with no routing context. The test 'propagates provider errors with routing context' (line 309) is misleadingly named -- it only verifies the raw error propagates, not that routing context is added. Debugging which tier/provider failed in production requires manual correlation. The error_handling rules require 'include context in errors: what failed, why, what input caused it'.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the provider call in a try/catch that enriches the error: catch (error) { throw new Error(`ModelRouter: ${tier} provider failed for ${taskType} task: ${error.message}`, { cause: error }) }. Update the test name to match the actual behavior.

findings.title: Override to 'local' tier silently falls back when local provider absent
findings.description: When routingOverrides explicitly maps a task to 'local' but no local provider is configured, resolveTier() returns 'local' (line 98 honors the override) and getProvider() silently falls back to lightweight via the ?? operator (line 180). The user's explicit routing configuration is silently ignored with no warning, log, or error. This could cause unexpected cost increases if the user believes classification calls are going to a free local model but they're actually hitting the paid lightweight tier.
findings.suggestion: Either (a) validate in the constructor that any tier referenced in routingOverrides has a corresponding provider, or (b) log a warning when getProvider falls back.

findings.title: generateText and generateStructured share identical routing logic
findings.description: Both generateText (lines 135-144) and generateStructured (lines 146-159) perform the same 4-step routing sequence: classifyTask -> resolveTier -> getProvider -> increment stats. The only differences are (1) classifyTask receives an optional schemaDescription in generateStructured, and (2) they delegate to different provider methods. This is a 6-line near-duplicate block.
findings.suggestion: Extract a private route() method that centralizes routing + stats logic. Both generateText and generateStructured become one-liners delegating to the routed provider.

findings.title: Test setup (createTrackingProvider + new ModelRouter) repeated 12 times
findings.description: The 4-line pattern of creating lightweight + standard tracking providers and a ModelRouter appears in 10 of 14 test cases (lines 71-75, 88-92, 105-109, 121-125, 174-179, 194-199, 213-216, 235-239, 255-259, 293-296). This is well above the 3-occurrence threshold for extraction.
findings.suggestion: Extract a createRouter() test factory function that eliminates ~40 lines of repeated setup and makes test intent clearer by highlighting what differs (local provider, overrides) rather than what is always the same.

findings.title: Missing test: getProvider local-to-lightweight fallback path
findings.code_snippet:
case 'local':
  return this.providers.local ?? this.providers.lightweight
findings.description: The getProvider method has a fallback from local to lightweight when local is undefined (line 180). However, there is no test that exercises this specific fallback path. If an override forces tier='local' but no local provider is configured, the ?? fallback would be the only safety net, and it is untested.
findings.suggestion: Add a test: configure routingOverrides to force classification to 'local' tier, but do not provide a local provider. Verify that the call falls back to the lightweight provider and succeeds.

findings.title: Incomplete error path coverage for ModelRouter
findings.description: The error handling section has only one test case (provider throws Error). Missing error scenarios: (1) provider throws non-Error (string, undefined), (2) provider returns a rejected promise with a typed error, (3) error from standard provider (current test only fails the lightweight provider). These paths exercise different code branches since generateText and generateStructured both delegate to different providers.
findings.suggestion: Add at least one more error test: a failing standard provider (for generation/summarization tasks) to verify errors propagate from both tiers.

findings.title: Missing test: classifyTask with ambiguous/multi-keyword prompts
findings.description: classifyTask is a pure function with keyword-priority-based classification, but no test verifies behavior when a prompt contains keywords from multiple categories. The implementation has a specific priority order (classification > extraction > summarization > generation), which is a critical business decision that should be explicitly tested.
findings.suggestion: Add tests for ambiguous prompts to verify priority order: e.g., 'Classify and summarize the user activity' should return 'classification'.

findings.title: Performance benchmark memory assertions are vacuously true
findings.code_snippet:
expect(overheadMB).toBeLessThan(100)
...
expect(overheadMB).toBeLessThan(500)
findings.description: The memory budget tests assert that creating a ModelRouter + DeduplicationTracker uses less than 100MB (lightweight) or 500MB (full). These objects consist of a few counters and function references -- they use kilobytes at most. The assertions will never fail because the actual overhead is ~0.001-0.1MB, making the thresholds 1000x-500000x too generous. These tests provide false confidence about memory budgets.
findings.suggestion: Either tighten the bounds to realistic values (e.g., <1MB for lightweight, <5MB for full) or replace RSS-based tests with design-level assertions that verify no large buffers or caches are created.

findings.title: RoutingStats output interface lacks readonly modifiers
findings.description: RoutingStats is a read-only output from getStats() but all fields are mutable. This regresses from Task 19's readonly standard (DocumentExtractionResult, TextChunk, FileChangeEvent). Consumers can accidentally mutate the returned stats object's nested Records.
findings.suggestion: Add readonly modifiers to all fields: `readonly totalCalls: number`, `readonly callsByTier: Readonly<Record<ModelTier, number>>`, etc.

findings.title: DeduplicationStats output interface lacks readonly modifiers
findings.description: DeduplicationStats is returned from getStats() as a snapshot and should be immutable. All four fields are mutable. Regresses from Task 19's readonly output types.
findings.suggestion: Add readonly modifiers to all fields. Consider a branded type or type alias like `type Ratio = number` for the 0-1 deduplicationRate field.

findings.title: routingOverrides can specify 'local' tier without a local provider
findings.code_snippet: routingOverrides?: Partial<Record<TaskType, ModelTier>>
findings.description: The routingOverrides field accepts any ModelTier value including 'local', but the local provider is optional. If a consumer configures `routingOverrides: { summarization: 'local' }` without providing a local provider, getProvider() silently falls back to the lightweight provider. This violates the principle of making illegal states unrepresentable. The constructor does not validate that override tiers have corresponding providers.
findings.suggestion: Add constructor validation: iterate over routingOverrides entries and throw if any override specifies 'local' when `providers.local` is undefined.

findings.title: Keyword-based task classification is fragile and order-dependent
findings.description: The classifyTask function relies on substring matching against hardcoded keyword lists with priority ordering. A system prompt containing both 'classify' and 'extract' would always be classified as 'classification' due to check order. The keyword 'tag' could match unrelated words in prompts (e.g., 'vintage'). This is acceptable for an initial implementation but will become a maintenance burden as prompt templates evolve.
findings.suggestion: Consider this acceptable for now but add a comment documenting the priority semantics. For future iterations, a weighted scoring approach would be more robust. Add test cases for multi-keyword prompts to document priority behavior.

findings.title: Inline comments in resolveTier restate obvious code logic
findings.description: The inline comments '// Check explicit override first' and '// Route to local if available and task is eligible' directly restate what the code already expresses through its variable names and conditional logic. The function already has a JSDoc comment explaining its purpose.
findings.suggestion: Consider removing these comments since the JSDoc and self-documenting code already convey the intent. Minor style preference.

findings.title: Comment restates code in test: '// no local provider'
findings.code_snippet: // no local provider
findings.description: The comment '// no local provider' restates what is obvious from the constructor call above it, which only passes lightweight and standard providers. The test name already communicates this intent.
findings.suggestion: Remove the comment. The test name and constructor call make the absence of a local provider self-evident.

findings.title: Magic number 5 in areSimilar default threshold
findings.description: The default threshold of 5 in areSimilar is a magic number representing 'maximum Hamming distance for similarity on a 64-bit hash' but is not given a named constant. The codebase already defines HASH_SIZE = 8 as a named constant in the same file.
findings.suggestion: Extract to a named constant: `const DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 5` with a JSDoc explaining the 64-bit hash context.

findings.title: Image pixel generation loops repeated 3 times in dedup-stats test
findings.description: The beforeAll block generates 3 test images using nearly identical nested loops (lines 109-148). Each loop iterates pixels and sets RGB values with only the color values differing. A helper would reduce ~24 lines to ~12 lines.
findings.suggestion: Extract a makeGradient(w, h, rgbFn) helper that takes a color function and returns a Buffer, eliminating 3 near-identical 8-line loop blocks.

findings.title: Interface compliance test is trivially weak
findings.description: This test checks that ModelRouter has generateText and generateStructured methods by verifying typeof is 'function'. This is already guaranteed by TypeScript's 'implements LlmProvider' clause at compile time. The test name says 'integration with ContextMerger' but does not test integration with ContextMerger.
findings.suggestion: Either remove this test (TypeScript already enforces interface compliance) or make it a real integration test that instantiates a ContextMerger with a ModelRouter and verifies end-to-end behavior.

findings.title: ModelRouter.generateStructured<T> inherits unconstrained generic
findings.description: ModelRouter faithfully passes through the unconstrained generic T from LlmProvider.generateStructured. Callers get no compile-time guarantee that the LLM output matches T. Consumers of ModelRouter must remember to validate outputs themselves, following the SmartTip/MemoryExtractor pattern.
findings.suggestion: Informational. Consumers should always add runtime validation. Consider adding a JSDoc warning on the method.

positive_observations: Clean Functional Core / Imperative Shell separation: classifyTask and resolveTier are pure functions, ModelRouter is the imperative shell
Test doubles use the correct pattern with explicit 'Test Double rationale' comments for external LLM API boundaries
DeduplicationTracker handles division-by-zero gracefully (returns 0 when no comparisons)
ModelRouter implements LlmProvider interface enabling transparent drop-in usage
Good test coverage: pure function tests for classifyTask, routing behavior tests, stats tracking, error propagation, and performance benchmarks
The performance benchmark test validates sub-millisecond routing overhead and O(1) tracker operations
getProvider has a sensible fallback chain: local -> lightweight when local is unavailable
JSDoc on all exported interfaces and functions follows project conventions consistently
The RoutingStats.estimatedSavingsRatio JSDoc explains both the formula and the interpretation, which is high-value documentation
Section divider comments (// --- Functional Core --- etc.) align with the project's FC/IS architecture
The simulation scenario comment block in dedup-stats.test.ts effectively explains the 'why' behind the test design
DeduplicationTracker is a clean pure-data class with no I/O -- error-free by design
getStats() returns shallow copies of internal state, preventing external mutation
Division-by-zero is correctly guarded in both getStats() and DeduplicationTracker.getStats()
No vi.fn(), vi.mock(), jest.fn(), or jest.mock() violations detected -- all test doubles are hand-crafted interface implementations
classifyTask (Functional Core) is tested as a pure function with direct instantiation, no mocks needed
DeduplicationTracker tests include excellent boundary conditions: zero comparisons, all duplicates, all unique, and reset behavior
dedup-stats.test.ts includes a real integration test using sharp image processing with computePHash
TaskType and ModelTier are proper literal unions, consistent with codebase best practices
LOCAL_ELIGIBLE_TASKS uses ReadonlySet -- good immutability pattern for constant configuration
All functions are well under 30 lines, matching the project's established complexity thresholds
Named constants HASH_SIZE, CLASSIFICATION_KEYWORDS etc. follow project convention for avoiding magic values
